author: Авакумов Данил Эдуардович
objective: Test whether the discovered cooperative law reflects survival dynamics rather than direct weight causality by injecting survival-pressure scaling into the selection process.

previous_causal_test.interpretation: Direct weight injection does not improve system performance, suggesting the law reflects emergent survival dynamics rather than causal weight control.

background.hypothesis: The empirical law approximates role survival probability under selection pressure rather than optimal fixed weight allocation.

research_hypothesis.statement: Injecting survival-pressure scaling into fitness evaluation will improve cooperative dynamics if the discovered law approximates role survival probability.

experimental_regimes.description: Standard cooperative coevolution without survival-pressure scaling.
Fitness scaled using discovered survival law.
Fitness scaled only by variance term: fitness × sigma_r^alpha.
Fitness scaled only by inverse mean signal: fitness × (1 / mu_r^beta).

experiment_blocks.title: SURVIVAL-PRESSURE IMPLEMENTATION
experiment_blocks.goal: Extend coevolution engine to support survival-pressure scaling during selection.

experiment_blocks.title: MULTI-REGIME EXECUTION
experiment_blocks.goal: Run experiments under baseline and three survival-pressure regimes.

experiment_blocks.title: STABILITY ANALYSIS
experiment_blocks.goal: Analyze role ratio stability and fitness variance across generations.

experiment_blocks.title: MECHANISM VALIDATION
experiment_blocks.goal: Determine whether survival-pressure scaling improves cooperative regime stability.

experiment_blocks.title: FINAL REPORT
experiment_blocks.goal: Interpret experimental results and determine whether the discovered law reflects survival dynamics.

definition_of_done: Survival-pressure scaling implemented in coevolution engine.
All regimes executed across N=30 seeds.
